channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - remapradio.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for remapradio.com

item.title: This Week at Remap
item.content:encoded:
On last week's (delightful) platformer stream, I promised that we'd include a link to a form and community doc that'd help us pick the final game for this next month's slate, and then...did not put the links there! Apologies. The links are available for you below:

  • Form
  • A Formerly Chaotic Doc That Is Now a Calm Community

We'll, of course, spin a wheel tomorrow morning to determine the last game that'll become part of our ranking slate. If you missed it, the games that were doing ahead of the next platformer podcast are...

  • Chameleon Twist
  • Mischief Makers
  • Glover
  • Yoshi's Story
  • [UNANNOUNCED FIFTH N64 GAME BUT I MAY BE QUIETLY ROOTING FOR BLUES BROTHERS 2000]
  • All-Timer: Super Mario World

-pk, who is very anxious to start chatting about Mina the Hollower

  • PODCAST: My Turn: Logan Lucky (feat: Rob, Patrick, Janet, Chia) – This episode of My Turn was originally a Premium Subscriber only episode, but we're releasing it to everyone while we're taking a break for this week's holiday! If you'd like to listen to more episodes of My Turn, including the upcoming episode on Ocean's Eleven, you can sign up for a library tier subscription at https://remapradio.com/signup
  • STREAM: Zero Parades (feat: Rob, Chia) – Politics, spies, and streaming?
  • STREAM: Platformer Stream (feat: Janet, Patrick, Chia) – Like I said, we'll announce/pick the final selection from our ~~weird N64~~, dive into Super Mario World, and maybe poke around at a few newer platformers.
  • ARTICLE/PODCAST: Mina the Hollower – This interview was a jam.
  • PODCAST: Remap Radio – I've played a lot of Mina the Hollower, and was hoping I'd have spent a little time with James Bond, but we'll have to see.
  • ARTICLE: Letter Series – Topic TBD
  • STREAM: Blue Prince – The puzzle "masters" are, in theory, at it again.

If you have any support issues, send details to support@remapradio.com

Patrick Klepek (he/him) is an editor at Remap. In another life, he worked on horror movie sets, but instead, he also runs Crossplay, a newsletter about parenting and video games. You can follow him on Twitter, Threads, Mastodon, and Bluesky.
